Technology.headline: Nvidia in Orbit? Job Listing Suggests GPU Maker Eyeing Space-Based Data Centers
Technology.detail: A job posting indicates Nvidia might be exploring the possibility of building AI-focused data centers in space. The California-based system architect would 'help define and build products for AI in orbit.'

Technology.headline: Apple launches MacBook Neo with A18 Pro chip and 13-inch display, price starts at Rs 69,990
Technology.detail: Apple has released the MacBook Neo, featuring the A18 Pro chip and a 13-inch display, with a starting price of Rs 69,990. This new model aims to provide a more affordable entry point into the MacBook ecosystem.

Technology.headline: SpaceX's Starlink Mobile Gets a Boost From Chipmaker MediaTek
Technology.detail: MediaTek is partnering with SpaceX to bring direct-to-cell satellite communication capabilities to Starlink Mobile. This collaboration aims to improve connectivity for mobile devices in remote areas.

Technology.headline: EU experts to start work on social media ban for children
Technology.detail: Experts in the European Union are beginning to explore the possibility of implementing a ban on social media for children. This initiative is driven by concerns over the potential negative impacts of social media on young users' mental health and well-being.

Technology.headline: Hands On With the MacBook Pro M5: Apple Hides a Monster Inside
Technology.detail: The new MacBook Pro features Apple's latest M5 Pro and M5 Max chips, offering a significant performance boost. These new chips represent a radical fusion of silicon technology, making the MacBook Pro a powerhouse.

Science.headline: Irrigation gaps in weather models could skew air quality forecasts, study finds
Science.detail: Researchers have found that irrigation practices, when not accurately represented in weather models, can lead to skewed air quality forecasts due to the impact on atmospheric conditions.

Science.headline: GLP-1 medications get at the heart of addiction, study finds
Science.detail: A recent study suggests that GLP-1 medications, typically used for weight loss and diabetes, may also have potential in treating addiction by targeting the underlying mechanisms in the brain.

Science.headline: Quitting GLP-1 drugs triggers rapid regain, but 25% of weight loss may last
Science.detail: Research indicates that while individuals often experience rapid weight regain after discontinuing GLP-1 drugs, a significant portion, around 25%, may sustain their weight loss.

Science.headline: First gene regulation clinical trials for epilepsy show promising results
Science.detail: Early clinical trials focusing on gene regulation as a treatment for epilepsy have demonstrated encouraging outcomes, suggesting a potential new avenue for managing the condition.

Science.headline: Most compact quadruple star system yet fits within an area the size of Jupiter's orbit
Science.detail: Astronomers have discovered an extremely compact quadruple star system, where all four stars are contained within a space comparable to the orbit of Jupiter.

Health.headline: How the fats we eat shape our ability to fight disease
Health.detail: The types of fats we consume directly impacts the survival and strength of the body's immune cells and ability to fight disease, researchers have found, indicating that dietary changes could potentially boost the effectiveness of vaccines and cancer therapies. A diet with a lower ratio of polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFAs) to monounsaturated fatty acids (MUFAs) makes T cells much more resilient and resistant to cell death.

Health.headline: Avoid extreme, harmful diets, weight experts say
Health.detail: A study conducted last year showed that more than 40 percent of Taiwanese who tried to lose weight resorted to “extreme diets” and other regimens that were harmful to health. Proper diet, regular exercise and treatment by medical professionals are the three essential components of a healthy weight loss regimen.

Health.headline: Chile becomes the first country in the Americas to be verified by WHO for the elimination of leprosy
Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO), together with the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO), congratulates Chile for becoming the first country in the Americas to be officially verified as having eliminated leprosy disease. Chile has not reported any locally acquired case of leprosy for more than 30 years, with the last locally acquired case detected in 1993.

Health.headline: Mobile Mammogram Screenings - March 6, 2026
Health.detail: The Florida Department of Health in Citrus County will be offering mobile mammogram screenings on March 6, 2026, from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. at Langley Health Services. The screenings are free for women ages 40-64 who meet specific income and insurance criteria.
Health.source: Florida Department of Health in Citrus County

Health.headline: NHS facing 'second surge' in vomiting virus as cases reach highest level this winter
Health.detail: The NHS could be facing a “second surge” in norovirus as the vomiting bug reaches its highest level so far this winter. Figures published today show the average number of patients in hospital with norovirus each day this week rose to over 1,000 for the first time this winter (1,012).

Sports.headline: Allen's record-breaking century earns New Zealand big win over South Africa in T20 World Cup semis
Sports.detail: Finn Allen slammed a 33-ball century as New Zealand thrashed South Africa by 9 wickets to reach the finals of the T20 World Cup. Allen's historic ton guided New Zealand to the T20 World Cup Final with a win against South Africa.

Sports.headline: MLB: Jurickson Profar banned for 2026 season after second failed drug test
Sports.detail: Atlanta Braves' Jurickson Profar suspended for 2026 season following second positive drug test. Profar will be suspended for the entire 2026 season due to the violation.

Sports.headline: Chiefs reportedly trade All-Pro corner McDuffie to Rams for first-round pick
Sports.detail: The Kansas City Chiefs have reportedly traded All-Pro cornerback Trent McDuffie to the Los Angeles Rams in exchange for a first-round pick. This move comes as a surprise, with McDuffie being a key player for the Chiefs' defense.

Sports.headline: Manchester United's Jack Fletcher given six-match ban for homophobic slur
Sports.detail: Jack Fletcher, son of Manchester United's Darren Fletcher, has been suspended for six games for making a homophobic insult about an opponent. The suspension highlights the ongoing efforts to combat discrimination in sports.

Sports.headline: Luke Donald to return as Ryder Cup captain for Europe for 2027 competition
Sports.detail: Luke Donald will return as Ryder Cup captain for Europe for the 2027 competition. This decision comes after his successful leadership in the previous Ryder Cup.

Entertainment.headline: Actor Awards 2026 Recap: Must-See Moments and What You Didn't See on TV at SAG Celebration
Entertainment.detail: The Actor Awards 2026 saw many must-see moments and had a lot of behind-the-scenes action at the SAG Celebration.

Entertainment.headline: Rashmika blushes as Vijay says, 'Telugu states have a new daughter-in-law'
Entertainment.detail: Actor Vijay made comments about Rashmika during their wedding reception.

Entertainment.headline: Beckham Family Feud: What Brooklyn Accused David & Victoria of & More
Entertainment.detail: Brooklyn Beckham has accused his parents, David and Victoria Beckham, of manipulating their public image throughout his life, leading to a family feud.

Entertainment.headline: Robert De Niro recites Abraham Lincoln's warning call for 'civility' at Carnegie Hall
Entertainment.detail: Robert De Niro recited Abraham Lincoln's warning call for 'civility' at Carnegie Hall.

Entertainment.headline: Joe Walsh on custom guitars, craftsmanship and letting go | AP interview
Entertainment.detail: Joe Walsh, the famed guitarist and Rock & Roll Hall of Famer, is auctioning off some 800 items to benefit his military veterans charity.

Education.headline: B.C.'s students falling behind in math skills, report finds
Education.detail: A report indicates that students in British Columbia are falling behind in math skills due to factors like the vague math curriculum.

Education.headline: Alberta teachers go to court seeking injunction on Charter notwithstanding case
Education.detail: Alberta teachers are seeking an injunction in court related to a Charter notwithstanding case.

Education.headline: Marfa schools going back to 5 days a week
Education.detail: Marfa ISD will return to a five-day calendar for the 2026-2027 school year, a change from the four-day calendar implemented in 2023, to increase instructional days due to the district's school improvement status.

Education.headline: Student Data Privacy: What Happens After Graduation?
Education.detail: An article discusses student data privacy and what happens to that data after a student graduates.

Education.headline: Addressing Chronic Absenteeism: Six Key Elements Every School Leader Should Include in Their Plans.
Education.detail: The article highlights six key elements that school leaders should include in their plans to address chronic absenteeism.

Lifestyle.headline: Blooming brilliant: 63 ways to spring into spring
Lifestyle.detail: The Guardian provides a list of 63 ideas to celebrate the arrival of spring, including gardening, outdoor activities, and recipes.

Lifestyle.headline: Pokemon truck with Switch 2 game stations and sofas around Singapore till June
Lifestyle.detail: To celebrate the launch of the new Pokemon Pokopia game on the Nintendo Switch 2, a travelling Pokemon truck will make its way across Singapore from Mar 5 to Jun 4.

Lifestyle.headline: Hari Raya 2026: 10 Singapore and Malaysia labels to shop for your festive outfits
Lifestyle.detail: Planning your Raya 2026 looks? These labels offer everything from baju kurungs, kebayas, kaftans and versatile separates that balance modesty, comfort and style for the festivities.

Lifestyle.headline: Pass notes. The posh egg problem: how they became a status symbol – and shoplifting target.
Lifestyle.detail: The Guardian discusses the increasing popularity and high value of certain types of eggs, leading to them becoming a status symbol and target for shoplifting.

Lifestyle.headline: Why Indian Women Struggle To Lose Weight After 30, Mumbai Doctor Weighs In
Lifestyle.detail: An article in NDTV discusses the reasons why Indian women find it difficult to lose weight after the age of 30, with insights from a Mumbai-based doctor.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Urgent: Save BARMM
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Marcos administration needs to act decisively to prevent the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) from deteriorating into a crisis due to toxic politics and incompetence, especially with the US-Israel war on Iran exacerbating existing challenges.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Reshaping reflects resilience
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Amidst escalating trade frictions and a sluggish domestic economy, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz's visit to China signals a changed mentality in Europe, emphasizing the importance of a stable and progressive Sino-German relationship built on mutual respect and economic cooperation.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: 'Two sessions' serve as important window for understanding China's governance: Global Times editorial
Opinion/Editorial.detail: China's annual sessions of the NPC and the CPPCC offer a crucial glimpse into the country's political and economic trends, showcasing how it addresses diverse social demands and transforms public opinion into effective governance, setting an example of democratic governance.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Letters to The Editor — March 5, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The letters discuss global consequences from the strikes on Iran, which include loss of lives, flight cancellations, and a looming energy crisis.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Critical juncture in AI governance
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The UN has established the Independent International Scientific Panel on Artificial Intelligence (AI), signaling a move towards more institutionalized AI governance, however classification of AI risks might well translate into future technological trade barriers.

World.headline: US Submarine Sinks Iranian Warship as Conflict Spreads Beyond Middle East
World.detail: The US military confirms that one of its submarines sank an Iranian warship in international waters as the conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran intensifies across the region. This action is part of a broader series of strikes and counter-strikes disrupting stability and raising fears of a wider war.

World.headline: NATO Shoots Down Iranian Missile Headed to Turkey
World.detail: NATO forces intercepted and destroyed an Iranian missile en route to Turkey, marking a significant escalation in the conflict. Debris from the intercepted missile landed on Turkish soil.

World.headline: US Senate Blocks Bid to Limit Trump's War Powers on Iran
World.detail: The US Senate has voted against a resolution that aimed to restrict President Trump's ability to wage war against Iran, with Republicans largely supporting the President's actions. The vote highlights the ongoing debate in the US regarding the extent of presidential war powers.

World.headline: Spain Denies White House Claim of Cooperating with US Military on Iran
World.detail: Despite claims from the White House, Spain's Foreign Minister Jose Manuel Albares has refuted that Spain has agreed to cooperate with the US military in regards to the conflict with Iran. This comes after Trump threatened trade sanctions against Spain for its stance against the US-Israeli strikes on Iran.

World.headline: Death Toll in Iran Surpasses 1,000 as Israel-US Strikes Continue
World.detail: As the conflict escalates, the death toll in Iran has exceeded 1,000 due to ongoing strikes by the US and Israel. The situation remains volatile with increasing concerns over civilian casualties and regional stability.

Zimbabwe.headline: Gunmen storm church, flee with cash and laptops
Zimbabwe.detail: Armed robbers raided a church, making off with cash and electronic devices.

Zimbabwe.headline: Armed robbers loot Mukuru outlet in Bulawayo
Zimbabwe.detail: A Mukuru outlet in Bulawayo was targeted by armed robbers who stole an undisclosed amount of money.

Zimbabwe.headline: Businessman dumps estranged wife's belongings in classroom
Zimbabwe.detail: A businessman is reported to have dumped his estranged wife's belongings in a classroom.

Zimbabwe.headline: Shadowy group threatens Madhuku, Biti
Zimbabwe.detail: A shadowy group has reportedly issued threats against Madhuku and Biti, raising concerns about political intimidation.

Zimbabwe.headline: Gokwe Chief Sounds Alarm Over Rising Adultery Cases
Zimbabwe.detail: A Gokwe Chief has voiced concerns over the increasing number of adultery cases in the region.
